profile.name: Mathan Kumar P
profile.title: Assistant Professor of Physics
profile.bio: Leading interdisciplinary research at the intersection of artificial intelligence and molecular biology. Focused on developing computational methods to understand complex biological systems and disease mechanisms.

experience.title: Senior Research Scientist
experience.organization: Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
experience.description: Leading computational biology research group focusing on single-cell genomics and machine learning applications in cancer biology.

experience.title: Postdoctoral Researcher
experience.description: Developed novel algorithms for analyzing high-dimensional biological data and integrative genomics approaches.

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.description: Worked on computational methods for phylogenetic analysis and evolutionary genomics.

academic.description: Dissertation: "Machine Learning Approaches for Understanding Gene Regulatory Networks"
Specialization in Machine Learning and Data Mining
Double major with minor in Mathematics

research.title: Single-Cell Atlas of Cancer
research.description: Building a comprehensive single-cell reference atlas of tumor microenvironments across cancer types.

research.title: Deep Learning for Drug Discovery
research.description: Developing graph neural networks for molecular property prediction and drug-target interaction.

research.title: Gene Regulatory Networks
research.description: Inference of regulatory relationships from multi-omics data using causal discovery methods.

publications.title: Comprehensive integration of single-cell data
publications.description: A framework for integrating diverse single-cell datasets to enable comparative analysis across experiments and conditions.

publications.title: Spatial transcriptomics reveals tissue architecture
publications.authors: Chen S., Johnson R., et al.
publications.description: High-resolution spatial mapping of gene expression in complex tissues using advanced sequencing technologies.

events.title: Keynote: AI in Precision Medicine
events.location: International Conference on Computational Biology, Boston, MA
events.description: Presented recent advances in machine learning applications for personalized cancer treatment.

events.title: RECOMB 2023
events.description: Presented poster on "Scalable algorithms for single-cell multi-omics integration".

jobs.title: Postdoctoral Researcher
jobs.description: Seeking a motivated postdoc to work on single-cell multi-omics integration methods. Strong programming skills in Python/R required.

jobs.title: Graduate Student
jobs.description: Looking for PhD students interested in computational biology and machine learning. Rotation students welcome.
